description_long: This fence is constructed from interwoven wooden slats, creating a classic wattle or woven pattern. The horizontal slats are tightly woven between a series of evenly spaced vertical posts, giving the structure both flexibility and strength. The wood appears to be untreated or lightly finished, maintaining a natural, light tan color throughout. The top edge of the fence is slightly undulating, following the natural rise and fall of the woven slats, while the vertical posts extend just above the weave, providing a subtle accent. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, emphasizing a rustic and utilitarian design. The open weave allows for partial visibility and airflow, making it suitable for gardens, parks, or as a low urban barrier.
description: A rustic woven wooden fence with interlaced horizontal slats and evenly spaced vertical posts, featuring a natural tan color and an undulating top edge.
description_view_0: A narrow side profile showing the thinness of the woven structure and the alignment of the vertical posts.
description_view_1: A full frontal view displaying the woven pattern of horizontal slats and the evenly spaced vertical posts, with the undulating top edge clearly visible.
description_view_2: Another narrow side profile, similar to view 0, highlighting the compactness and thickness of the fence.
description_view_3: A rear view nearly identical to the front, showing the same woven pattern and post arrangement.
